title: Tenant / Workspace Production Guarantee
decision_rationale: GL-215 implements narrow tenant/workspace hardening for secondary execution-derived routes and demo tamper mutation while preserving Developer Preview / Controlled Preview boundaries and documenting remaining production blockers.

current_tenant_workspace_state_summary.tenant_id: Server-derived from operator row or demo admin-token context; primary routes filter by tenant.
current_tenant_workspace_state_summary.workspace_id: Reserved and nullable; workspace enforcement remains deferred.
current_tenant_workspace_state_summary.admin_operator: Operators are tenant-bound and admin-only operator creation assigns tenant IDs; not a full production tenant lifecycle system.
current_tenant_workspace_state_summary.audit: Tenant context is propagated for applicable audit events; workspace remains nullable.

production_tenant_workspace_gap_assessme: Workspace enforcement remains a production blocker.
Tenant provisioning and workspace-management APIs are not implemented.
Database row-level security is not implemented.
Admin tenant lifecycle governance is not production-complete.
Secondary helper-level tenant parameters remain future work even though GL-215 guards HTTP routes first.
implemented_hardening_summary: Added tenant-visible execution pre-checks to evidence, evidence export, evidence verification, provenance summary, auditor report, evidence completeness, and compliance gap execution routes.
Tenant-scoped demo tamper grant route and helper to deny cross-tenant direct-ID mutation.
No schema, migration, broad auth, RBAC, persistence, SDK, package, public publish, or workflow changes.
tenant_id_derivation_model: Operator mode derives tenant_id from the authenticated operator row. Admin-token mode derives tenant_id=demo server-side. Headers and ordinary business bodies do not establish tenant context.
workspace_id_derivation_deferred_model: workspace_id remains reserved and nullable. GL-215 does not enforce workspace membership or workspace-scoped queries; null workspace_id does not broaden tenant access.
cross_tenant_lookup_denial_behavior: Primary direct-ID lookups remain tenant-filtered, and GL-215 extends 404 denial to secondary execution-derived routes before derived builders run.
cross_tenant_mutation_denial_behavior: Primary mutations remain tenant-filtered. Demo tamper and evidence verification now require tenant-visible records before mutation-capable behavior can run.
admin_operator_tenant_boundary: Operator tenant context is stored server-side and cannot be self-overridden. Admin operator creation may assign tenant IDs but is not a full production tenant-control system.
route_protection_filtering_model: Health/readiness stay public without tenant data. Business routes require auth and use server-derived tenant filtering. Secondary execution-derived routes now require tenant-visible execution IDs.
audit_tenant_workspace_propagation: Audit events include safe tenant context where applicable; raw tokens, token hashes, auth headers, DSNs, private keys, credentials, customer data, private grant data, and institutional data are excluded. Workspace remains nullable/deferred.
unsafe_override_prevention: Caller-provided tenant/workspace values on business routes are ignored for context derivation. Admin-only operator tenantId is explicit assignment for a new operator, not an operator override.
openapi_api_contract_implications: No OpenAPI change required; existing response shapes and 404 denial semantics are preserved.
migration_schema_implications: No migration or schema change. Workspace enforcement, row-level security, and tenant/workspace lifecycle schema remain future work.
production_readiness_impact: Materially reduces tenant/workspace risk but does not make GrantLayer production SaaS ready or real-data ready.
controlled_preview_impact: Controlled Preview remains limited to synthetic/demo data with strict boundaries.
remaining_tenant_workspace_blockers: Tenant and workspace provisioning/lifecycle management are missing.
Admin plane is not production tenant lifecycle governance.
Helper-level tenant parameters for all secondary evidence/report modules remain recommended future hardening.
Production data governance, legal, incident, and compliance gates remain incomplete.

risk_register.risk: Workspace-null records cannot prove workspace isolation.
Future routes could omit tenant context.
Secondary helpers rely on HTTP route pre-checks.
Admin tenant assignment lacks production tenant lifecycle controls.

findings: Secondary execution-derived HTTP routes needed tenant-visible execution pre-checks.
Demo tamper mutation needed server-derived tenant scoping.
Workspace enforcement remains too broad for GL-215 and is documented as a blocker.
